_description: TPG-GRAO Learning Component — logs known failures, suppresses retrying them, prioritizes known-working approaches. Integrates into the GRAO gradient-driven cycle.

components.name: Known-Failures Store
components.function: Central registry of config paths, methods, and workarounds that agents have learned. GRAO queries this before generating attempt proposals.
operations: log_failure: agent attempts failed method → record in store
mark_learned: agent adopts workaround → mark entry learned
suppress: GRAO auto-suppresses learned-failed methods from proposal queue
track_unlearned: agents still retrying known-failed paths flagged as unlearned

components.name: Learning Rate Tracker
components.function: Measures how quickly agents adopt known workarounds vs retrying failures.

components.name: SCAR Integration
components.function: SCAR files serve as the authoritative source for known failures. GRAO learns from SCAR entries without requiring agent trial-and-error.
components.flow: SCAR created → GRAO ingests → agents query SCAR first → failures reduced to zero

components.name: Gradient Integration
components.function: Learning rate becomes a GRAO gradient that drives agent behavior optimization.

gradients.action: force SCAR ingestion into agent context
check known-failures store → redirect to workaround if learned

implementation_steps: Step 3: GRAO cron updated to ingest known-failures before proposal generation
Step 4: Agents trained to query SCAR first before config patch attempts
Step 5: Learning rate tracking integrated into GRAO monitor
Step 6: Feedback loop — learning rate becomes optimization gradient
expected_outcomes: Zero token waste on known-failed config patch attempts
Agents consistently use known workarounds first
SCAR files become community knowledge base for all OpenClaw agents
TPG-GRAO learning gradient drives continuous optimization
